textPlain:
Summary of the change since it doesn’t outline the previous policy:This policy change tightens visa application rules significantly. Previously, applicants could generally apply for US nonimmigrant visas at any US embassy or consulate worldwide. The new policy requires applicants to apply in their country of nationality or residence, with several important consequences:Key changes:Location restriction: Must apply where you’re a national or resident, not just anywhere convenientProof of residence required: If applying based on residency (not nationality), you must demonstrate actual residencePractical barriers: Applying elsewhere becomes much harder - longer wait times, higher rejection likelihood, non-refundable feesExisting appointments preserved: Current scheduled interviews won’t be cancelledWhy this matters:This effectively ends “visa shopping” - the practice of applying at embassies with shorter wait times or perceived easier approval rates. It also prevents circumventing countries where the US has suspended visa operations.
Exceptions remain for diplomatic visas, UN-related travel, and emergency/humanitarian cases.This represents a significant tightening of administrative control over where visa applications can be processed, likely aimed at improving security screening and preventing abuse of the system.
Similar to Europe: for Schengen visas, you must apply to the consulate responsible for your country of legal residence
This is the organized and intentional destruction of the United States of America and it is revolting to watch.
Anything to make it more difficult.
